sizing_rule.note: Position size must not exceed the optimal f value calculated from historical win/loss ratios. The optimal f is the peak of the risk-to-gain curve where gain per unit of quantity at risk is maximized. Trading beyond optimal f results in mathematical certainty of ruin. Specific f value is system-dependent and must be calculated objectively from backtesting; no single universal value applies.

source_rule_text: Find the optimal f value at the peak of the risk-to-gain curve where you maximize potential gain per unit of quantity at risk; trading beyond this optimal f value results in mathematical certainty of ruin over time.
